description_long: This helicopter features a streamlined, elongated fuselage with a dark green and black color scheme, reminiscent of military utility helicopters such as the Bell UH-1 Iroquois (Huey). The main rotor consists of two long, slender blades with a yellow tip on one end, while the tail rotor is mounted on the left side of the tail boom. The cockpit is equipped with large, rectangular windows that wrap around the front and sides, providing excellent visibility for the pilots. The landing gear consists of two sturdy, tubular skids running parallel beneath the fuselage, connected by crossbars for stability. The helicopter's exterior appears to be constructed primarily from metal, with glass for the windows and some plastic or composite elements for smaller details.
description: A military-style helicopter with a dark green and black body, two-blade main rotor, side-mounted tail rotor, large cockpit windows, and tubular landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide cockpit windows, main rotor blades extending horizontally, and the landing skids beneath the nose.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, main and tail rotors, and the full length of the landing skids.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor on the left, and the tapering structure of the fuselage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the cockpit, fuselage, and landing skids from the other side.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, narrow main rotor blades, likely made of composite or metal, mostly black with a yellow tip on one blade; blades are straight and extend well beyond the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: Present on the left side of the tail boom, appears to have two blades oriented vertically, colored black with no visible markings or protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel, tubular metal skids painted gray, connected by crossbars for stability, extending the length of the lower fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Four large, rectangular cockpit windows wrapping around the front and sides, clear with a slight tint, framed in black, providing wide visibility.
